steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The sentence has one subject noun phrase — "Louisiana resident Oscar James Dunn, one of the nearly two thousand African Americans elected to public office during the decade that followed the Civil War" — and no main verb yet. The blank must supply a finite main verb to form a complete sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to begin': an infinitive, not a finite verb — cannot serve as a main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'having begun': a perfect participle, not a finite verb — creates a dangling modifier, not a main clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'beginning': a present participle, not a finite verb — same problem as B. ✗
  • Choice D — 'began': simple past finite verb — pairs correctly with the subject and the time reference "in 1868." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
